what_it_does: It is a read-only deep trace and refactor plan for `Suite.chat()` in `runtime/suite.py`, mapping its body into prologue, per-part core, and epilogue layers so that a shared core can be extracted for both `chat()` (N=1) and `chat_parts()` (N>1).
observations: The refactor boundary is deliberately placed below the return assembly, because `chat()` returns a single dict while `chat_parts()` streams N reply units.
The body is split into a turn-level prologue, a reusable per-part core, and a turn-level epilogue.
Off-mode and capability-gate refusal paths are early short-circuits with distinct return shapes and hardcoded provenance.
Normal-path user-turn append happens late, while off/refusal paths append early.
The capability gate refuses without fallback and without a model call.
`_chat_context` emits `warning` events, so it must be built once per turn rather than per part.
Existing tests monkeypatch `complete_with_tools` and `_model_supports_tools` at attribute level and assert exact confirmation substrings.
`chat_history(20)` reads the persisted log, so later parts in a turn must see earlier parts via a `run://` ref-read, not history.
The tool-loop, confirmation-fold, and `action_field` shaping region is the densest single-shot-coupled code and the main regression gate.
The highest-risk break is silent: off/refusal return shapes and provenance are not covered by the main regression test.
standouts: The extraction boundary sits below the return — this is the central design fact.
Trap 1/2: off/refusal short-circuits must stay outside the shared core or their return shapes and provenance silently change.
Trap 3: the test monkeypatch seam is the real regression gate; breaking it can make tests pass while prod breaks.
Trap 4: per-part `_chat_context` would multiply warning emits.
conventions: Uses file:line references and symbol names for anchoring.
Structures analysis as numbered sections with tables and labeled traps.
Distinguishes turn-level-once behavior from part-level-N behavior.
Enumerates exact preservation invariants for callers and tests.
concerns: Line-number anchors will drift as the source changes.
Several open design points are flagged but not resolved inside this file.
The off/refusal paths have no test coverage for their exact return shapes and provenance.
The tool-loop/confirmation-fold region is tightly coupled to single-shot semantics.
A per-part invocation of `_chat_context` could flood telemetry with duplicate warnings.
notes: A surgical pre-refactor trace of `Suite.chat()` that defines exactly where to cut a shared per-part core without breaking the single-shot return, event, and provenance contracts.
questions: What is the exact signature and return type of the proposed `_chat_part` shared core?
How will `action_field` and `proposals` be merged across N parts?
Will tool dispatch be allowed in every part or restricted to a final part?
How are resolved `run://` role values injected into later parts?
What is the intended ndjson framing and final turn epilogue for `chat_parts()`?
How is `is_final` determined for the last part?
What are the precise contents of `_mode_directive` and `_model_of_tim_digest`?
Is there a plan to add tests covering off/refusal return shapes and provenance before merge?
purpose_vs_actual: matches — the file states it is a detailed trace, extraction plan, preservation list, and risk inventory, and it delivers all of those.
intention_signals: It is trying to enable a multi-part chat API (`chat_parts()`) by extracting a reusable per-part cognitive core from `chat()` while keeping the existing single-shot `chat()` behavior byte-identical and safe against the existing regression suite.
novelty: The explicit framing that the extraction boundary must sit below the return, plus the catalog of single-shot assumptions as load-bearing traps rather than ordinary implementation details.
contribution: A structured decomposition (prologue / per-part core / epilogue), an exact preservation checklist for callers and tests, and a risk inventory that a fused system can use to guide the refactor and its regression gates.
summary_for_embedding: Detailed trace and refactor plan for `Suite.chat()` that splits it into turn-level prologue/epilogue and a reusable per-part shared core for `chat_parts()`, with preservation invariants and risk traps.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a safe extraction blueprint for a multi-part chat API while keeping existing `chat()` callers and regression tests unchanged.
